description.S: Chinese Braised Beef Shank is a traditional cold-sliced beef dish often served as an appetizer at Chinese banquet-style dinners. The beef shank is slowly braised in a fragrant soy-based master sauce until tender, then chilled overnight for clean slicing and deep flavor. It is wonderful on its own, garnished with sesame seeds and scallions, or used as a protein topping for noodle soup.

L.S: 5 Green Onion stalks, divided (3 for blanching, 2 for braising), cut into 3-inch segments
1 thumb-sized piece Fresh Ginger (about 1 oz) plus 3 slices, divided
1/3 cup plus 1 tablespoon Shaoxing Wine, divided
1 to 2 Dried Red Chilies, snipped in half
1 tablespoon Rock Sugar (or 2 teaspoons Brown Sugar)
1 tablespoon Toasted Sesame Seeds for garnish

note.S: Divided (3 for blanching, 2 for braising), cut into 3-inch segments
Fresh, thumb-sized (about 1 oz) plus 3 slices, divided

L.S: Trim excess surface fat from 2 to 2 1/2 pounds boneless beef shank. Cut into 2-3 roughly equal pieces, each about 3/4 to 1 pound. Place the beef in a pot and cover with cold water by at least 1 inch. Add 3 green onion segments, ginger slices, and 1 tablespoon Shaoxing wine. Bring to a boil over high heat. Skim the gray-brown foam that rises to the surface using a ladle or large spoon and discard it. Once boiling, reduce heat to medium and continue to boil for 8-10 minutes, skimming any remaining foam. Remove the beef and rinse under warm running water to wash off any clinging impurities. Discard the blanching liquid and aromatics.

Place the blanched beef in a small, deep, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven. Add 1/3 cup Shaoxing wine, 3 tablespoons light soy sauce, 1 1/2 tablespoons dark soy sauce, 3 whole star anise, 1 cinnamon stick, 3 bay leaves, 1 teaspoon fennel seeds, 3 whole cloves, 1 to 2 dried red chilies, 1 tablespoon rock sugar, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 2 green onion segments, and 3 slices fresh ginger. Roll and turn the beef in the sauce mixture for 1-2 minutes over medium-low heat so it is well coated and the aromatics begin to bloom. Add enough hot water to submerge the meat about two-thirds of the way, about 2-3 cups depending on pot size.

Increase heat to medium-high and bring to a boil. Once boiling, reduce heat to low for a gentle, lazy simmer with occasional small bubbles. Cover with a tight-fitting lid. Simmer for 2 hours. Every 30 minutes, uncover the pot and flip each piece of meat to ensure even coloring and flavor absorption. If the liquid reduces below halfway up the meat, add a splash of hot water. The beef is done when a chopstick or fork slides in with moderate resistance. The meat should hold its shape and not be falling apart, as you will need to slice it thinly when cold.

Turn off the heat. Leave the meat in the covered pot and allow it to cool for 1 to 1 1/2 hours at room temperature. Flip the meat once halfway through cooling. The beef will continue to absorb the braising flavors as it cools. Once the pot is cool enough to handle (within 2 hours of turning off heat), transfer the meat and braising liquid to a container and refrigerate.

Refrigerate the beef submerged in the braising liquid for at least 8 hours, preferably overnight. This firms up the meat for clean slicing and deepens the flavor. Remove the beef from the liquid. Slice against the grain as thinly as possible, about 1/8 inch or thinner, using a very sharp knife. Arrange slices on a plate. Garnish with 1 tablespoon toasted sesame seeds and 2 thinly sliced scallions. Optionally drizzle with a small amount of the braising liquid and/or chili oil. Serve cold or at room temperature.
Gluten-Free Substitution: Replace light soy sauce and dark soy sauce with gluten-free tamari. Replace Shaoxing wine with dry sherry.
Dairy-Free: This recipe is naturally dairy-free as written.
Instant Pot Shortcut: After blanching, add all braising ingredients plus 3 cups water. Pressure cook on high for 35 minutes, then natural release for 20 minutes.

Saving the braising liquid (master sauce): Strain the braising liquid and store it in the freezer for up to 3 months. It can be reused to braise more beef, pork, eggs, or tofu. Each use makes it more flavorful. Top off with a splash of fresh soy sauce, wine, and water when reusing.

Slicing tip: Always slice against the grain for the most tender result. If the beef is still too soft to slice thinly, let it chill longer or briefly place in the freezer for 20-30 minutes to firm up.
Substitute cuts: Beef chuck or brisket can be used if shank is unavailable, though the texture will differ.
Food safety: Transfer meat and braising liquid to the refrigerator within 2 hours of turning off the heat to avoid the temperature danger zone.
